title: Encoded PowerShell Spawned by Office Document
short_description: EDR fired on hoth-fs01 after WINWORD.EXE spawned an obfuscated PowerShell command moments after an email attachment was opened.

alert.description: powershell.exe was launched with -EncodedCommand by WINWORD.EXE on host hoth-fs01, approximately 90 seconds after the user opened an email attachment.
alert.raw: 06/12/2026 09:42:11 EDR-ALERT host=hoth-fs01 user=FROTHLY\fsmith rule="Encoded PowerShell Command Execution" process=powershell.exe parent=WINWORD.EXE severity=high

steps.title: Pull EDR process-creation events around the alert time
steps.spl: search index=endpoint host="hoth-fs01" earliest="2026-06-12T09:40:00" latest="2026-06-12T09:45:00" (Image="*powershell.exe" OR ParentImage="*WINWORD.EXE") | table _time, host, User, Image, ParentImage, ParentCommandLine, CommandLine

result.explanation: The -EncodedCommand argument is UTF-16LE Base64. Decoding it yields a single-line download cradle.

steps.title: Search network/proxy logs for connections to 185.220.101.47
steps.spl: search index=network (dest_ip="185.220.101.47" OR dest="185.220.101.47") host="hoth-fs01" earliest="2026-06-12T09:42:00" latest="2026-06-12T10:30:00" | stats count earliest(_time) as first_seen latest(_time) as last_seen avg(bytes_out) as avg_bytes by dest_ip, dest_port

events.note: Connections recur at ~60s intervals - consistent with C2 beaconing.

steps.title: Check DNS resolution history and threat intel for the destination
steps.spl: search index=dns (answer="185.220.101.47" OR dest_ip="185.220.101.47") host="hoth-fs01" | table _time, query, answer | append [| inputlookup threat_intel_domains.csv | search domain="cdn-update.net"]

steps.title: Look up asset context for hoth-fs01

result.notes: Hosts shared Finance drives; 40+ employees have SMB access to this host.

steps.title: Look up identity context for fsmith

result.title: Finance Analyst
result.recent_activity: Opened email attachment 'Q3_Vendor_Invoice.docm' from external sender 'billing@vendor-frothly-support.com' at 09:42:08, two minutes before the alert fired. No other anomalous activity on this account in the last 30 days.

ground_truth.narrative: A malicious macro inside 'Q3_Vendor_Invoice.docm' (delivered via a spoofed vendor email) launched an obfuscated PowerShell download cradle that retrieved a second-stage payload from 185.220.101.47 (cdn-update.net), a domain registered four days ago and already flagged by two threat-intel sources. The host then began beaconing to that address roughly once per minute. hoth-fs01 is a high-criticality Finance file server with broad SMB access, so this represents an active, ongoing compromise with a credible path to lateral movement across Finance data.
recommended_actions: Isolate host hoth-fs01 from the network via EDR containment immediately.
Block 185.220.101.47 and the domain cdn-update.net at the firewall, proxy, and DNS sinkhole.
Disable fsmith's account pending investigation and force a credential reset on re-enable.
Quarantine 'Q3_Vendor_Invoice.docm' across all mailboxes and block the sender domain vendor-frothly-support.com.
Hunt fleet-wide for the same indicators (cdn-update.net, the encoded PowerShell cradle, WINWORD.EXE spawning powershell.exe).
Open a critical incident with the IR team and capture a forensic image of hoth-fs01 before remediation.
